Anthopogon nervosum, commonly known as the Himalayan grass, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Poaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Anthopogon nervosum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, often used to treat respiratory and fever-related conditions.
